message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria (Indio)
Cuisine/type signal: Italian / pizzeria
Why it surfaced in discovery: Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella (relocated from successful NJ Italian restaurants); voted Coachella Valley's favorite Italian restaurant and favorite pizza; longtime independent on Hwy 111 — non-Mexican but a multi-decade Indio family-restaurant anchor.
Discovery sources: https://cirosindio.com/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/ciros-ristorante-&-pizzeria/6903/ | https://www.yelp.com/biz/ciros-ristorante-indio

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

I've found search results for Ciro's Ristorante Pizzeria in Indio with reviews from multiple platforms:

## Overview
Ciro's Ristorante was established in 1966, and it has been voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant and Favorite Pizza.

## Yelp Reviews
The Yelp page shows 344 reviews. Reviewers generally praise the pizza, with comments about their Margherita and Pepperoni pizzas. However, some recent reviews mention calling and getting a disconnected number, raising questions about whether they've reopened with another number or closed permanently.

## Google Reviews
Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria is ranked 4.3 within the Google grading system.

## Other Review Platforms
On TripAdvisor and other review sites, customers highlight:
- "The pizza has a really authentic New York tasting crust and the sauce is amazing"
- The restaurant offers an enjoyable dining experience with delicious food and great service, with Early Bird specials including tortellini marinara and chicken parmesan
- The restaurant offers a cozy atmosphere with excellent food and generous portion sizes, with friendly and attentive staff enhancing the dining experience

The restaurant is located at 81963 Hwy 111, Indio, CA 92201.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Indio area:

## Top Recommendations

Ciro's Pizza in Indio offers the best Italian food in town, voted Coachella Valley's favorite Italian restaurant and favorite pizza.

Mario's Italian Cafés has served the Coachella Valley for over 50 years with locations in Palm Desert, La Quinta, Indio, Bermuda Dunes, and Sun City Palm Desert, delighting residents with old-world family recipes and authentic Italian food and pizza.

Your Pie offers some of the best caprese pizza, sandwiches, and tomato mozzarella salads.

## Additional Options

Italica in Indio crafts a menu that leans into modern Italian cuisine with a focus on shareable, thoughtfully composed dishes.

Ristorante Mamma Gina, a historic eatery in the heart of Palm Desert, has been serving local desert patrons since 1986 and sits within the Gardens on El Paseo.

Trilussa, a lively restaurant in Cathedral City, offers chicken piccata, veal cacciatore and lasagna Bolognese, with locals raving about their olive tapenade.

# Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria

Based on the provided web page content, I cannot extract detailed information about Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria. The page appears to be a header/footer template from the Visit Greater Palm Springs tourism website, but it does not contain any actual content about Ciro's restaurant.

The page includes navigation menus, contact information for the Greater Palm Springs Convention & Visitors Bureau, partner logos, and social media links, but no restaurant-specific details such as:

- Description or cuisine type
- Hours of operation
- Address or phone number
- Historical background
- Awards or accolades

To find information about Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria in Indio, you would need to access the actual restaurant listing page or contact the Greater Palm Springs Visitor Center at **800.967.3767** or **760.770.9000**.

Based on the search results, here's the history of Ciro's Italian restaurant in Indio:

Ciro's Ristorante and Pizzeria was opened in 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella. The Mazzellas moved to the desert after many years as owners and operators of successful Italian Restaurants in New Jersey. Over the years other family members joined in the growth of the business.

The menu was developed by request of the customers, so everything that is available is tried and accepted over the many years. The restaurant was voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant and Favorite Pizza. Ron and Norma Hare continue the tradition today.

content.content:
# Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria - Indio

**Review Count:** 79 reviews

**Overall Rating:** 4.0 out of 5 bubbles

**Restaurant Ranking:** #22 of 165 restaurants in Indio

**Recent Sentiment (Last 12 Months):** Mixed to negative. The most recent review from August 2025 gave 2 of 5 bubbles, citing "cold food, unwanted onions, wrong pasta." The reviewer noted a $80 bill for substandard service and stated "we're unlikely to return."

**Ownership/Operations Changes:** The current owners, Ron and Norma Hare, "continue the tradition today" following the original founding by Anna and Jerry Mazzella in 1966. Multiple historical reviews reference management changes over the decades, with some earlier reviewers noting quality declined after ownership transitions.

**Closure Status:** No closure mentioned. Restaurant operates with standard hours: Sunday-Thursday 11am-8:30pm, Friday-Saturday until 9:30pm.

Based on the search results for the best pizza in Coachella Valley and Indio, here are some top recommendations:

Piero's in Indian Wells has a 4.3 rating and is described as having crispy crust and very flavorful toppings.

Upper Crust Pizza in Cathedral City is rated 3.8 and features thin-crust pizza that is nicely cooked with a toasty crust.

**In the Coachella Area:**

Popular pizza restaurants in Coachella include Sabatinos Pizza Pasta and Wings, TKB Bakery & Deli, Tuscano's & Brewquinta, Your Pie Pizza, and Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill.

Based on the search results, here's what I found for Italian restaurants in Indio, Coachella Valley suitable for date nights:

## Italica - Indio

Italica is an intimate hidden bar and restaurant serving outstanding Italian cuisine. Located at 82867 Miles Avenue Indio, CA 92201, this restaurant appears to be a perfect date night choice.

**Key Details:**
- Chef Santos crafts a menu that leans into modern Italian cuisine with a focus on shareable, thoughtfully composed dishes that encourage connection and conversation.
- Dishes are served tapas style and are meant to be combined and shared with others in your party.
- Italica is an adults-only establishment, and guests must be above the age of 18.
- Reservations are typically required for dinner service.
- Valet parking is now available Thursday, Friday & Saturday evenings.

**Reservations & Access:**
You'll enter through the big shiny silver door to the right of Indio Taphouse. Phone: (442) 400-3479

Italica's intimate atmosphere and focus on shareable dishes makes it an excellent choice for a romantic date night in the Coachella Valley.

content.text: I have enough information to assess. Let me compile the structured output.

input.name: Ciro's Ristorante & Pizzeria

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~344 reviews; TripAdvisor ~79 reviews; Restaurantji ~161 reviews. Google review count [UNVERIFIED] but rating reported ~4.3.
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive overall, but mixed-to-declining in recent reviews
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (2024-2025) show split signal: longtime fans still praise the NY-crust pizza, sauce, early bird specials and generous portions; a TripAdvisor August 2025 review (2/5) cited cold food, wrong pasta and poor value at ~$80/check; a Yelp Q&A thread questions whether the phone was disconnected (other reviewers note temporary closures during Coachella/Stagecoach when staff cannot keep up). No confirmed permanent closure.
reputation.longevity: Founded 1966 by Anna and Jerry Mazzella (relocated from NJ); ~60 years operating. Current operators Ron and Norma Hare continue the tradition. Ownership has transitioned from founding family but the brand has remained continuous.
credentials: Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant (self-reported / reflected on Visit Greater Palm Springs listing)
Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Pizza (self-reported / reflected on Visit Greater Palm Springs listing)
Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism listing

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Italian food in Indio' (named first on Coachella Valley Locals best-of list) and 'Italian restaurant Coachella Valley' (named in Visit Greater Palm Springs / search summaries as the favorite Italian and favorite pizza). Does NOT surface for 'best pizza Coachella Valley' top lists (TripAdvisor/Yelp lists return Piero's, SPC, Upper Crust, Sabatinos, Your Pie instead). Does NOT surface for 'date night Coachella Valley' (Italica and Palm Springs Life lists dominate). Absent from Palm Springs Life 'That's Amore' Italian roundup in search snippets.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Descriptions that do surface are largely accurate: 1966 founding, Mazzella family origin, NY-style crust, Hwy 111 Indio location, favorite Italian/pizza accolade. Some aggregator listings (Yellow Pages 'Ciros Restorante') incorrectly flag the business as CLOSED, and OpenTable carries two separate listings ('Ciro's Restorante' Italian and 'Ciro's' American) which create entity confusion.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Own domain cirosindio.com would not load via WebFetch (socket closed / 403) across multiple attempts — indicates the site is either intermittently available, blocking bots, or lightly maintained. Schema.org / JSON-LD presence [UNVERIFIED]. NAP signals are scattered across third-party aggregators (Yelp, TripAdvisor, OpenTable, Grubhub, Seamless, UberEats, SinglePlatform, Wheree, Restaurantji, Visit Greater Palm Springs, Facebook x2, Nextdoor) with inconsistent name spellings (Ciro's vs Ciros, Ristorante vs Restorante) and at least one stale 'CLOSED' flag on Yellow Pages.

competitors: Mario's Italian Cafes (multi-location CV chain, 50+ years, direct red-sauce Italian competitor incl. Indio)
Italica (Indio, modern shareable Italian, captures date-night queries Ciro's loses)
Piero's PizzaVino (Indian Wells, dominates 'best pizza CV' lists Ciro's is absent from)
Sabatinos / Your Pie / TKB Bakery (Indio-area Italian/pizza alternatives surfacing alongside Ciro's)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor (multi-decade independent on the Hwy 111 Indio dining strip; one of the oldest continuously operating sit-down restaurants in the eastern valley)

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owns a branded domain (cirosindio.com) — though it appears lightly maintained / bot-blocked. Active on Facebook (two separate pages: 'Ciro's Pizza Indio' and 'Ciros of Indio') suggesting page proliferation rather than managed social. Present on paid/free aggregators (Grubhub, Seamless, UberEats, OpenTable x2, SinglePlatform menu syndication, Yelp claimed) but no agency footprint visible, no PR/press placements in Palm Springs Life or Desert Sun surfacing in search, and no evidence of structured-data investment. Marketing spend appears low and reactive rather than strategic.
convertibility.reasoning: Independent family-operated restaurant (Hare family carrying on Mazzella tradition) with a reachable local decision-maker, 60-year continuous operation, and demonstrated willingness to invest in basic digital presence (own domain, multiple delivery integrations). The visibility gap is large: strong word-of-mouth reputation and a defensible 'favorite Italian / favorite pizza' anchor claim are not converting into top-of-list placement on pizza and date-night queries where newer competitors (Piero's, Italica) dominate, and an erroneous 'CLOSED' flag on at least one aggregator plus duplicate OpenTable listings are actively eroding agent trust. Clean structured data, consolidated listings, and a press/awards refresh would have outsized impact — classic convertible profile.

sources_consulted: https://cirosindio.com/ (WebFetch failed — socket closed / 403, multiple attempts)
WebSearch queries: best Italian Coachella Valley; best pizza Coachella Valley; date night Indio Italian; Ciro's closure status; Mazzella 1966 history; Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards
flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact Google review count and current Google star rating — search summary cited 4.3 but not directly fetched
[UNVERIFIED] Schema.org / JSON-LD presence on cirosindio.com — site would not respond to WebFetch across multiple attempts (403 / socket closed), which itself is a meaningful agent-visibility signal

[UNVERIFIED] 'Voted Coachella Valley's Favorite Italian Restaurant / Favorite Pizza' — claim appears on the Visit Greater Palm Springs listing and on the restaurant's own marketing but the specific award source/year/issuing publication (Desert Sun? Palm Springs Life? Coachella Valley Locals?) was not found in search; treat as self-reported until sourced

[UNVERIFIED] Current operating status — Yellow Pages flags one listing as CLOSED, Yelp Q&A thread mentions a disconnected phone number, but TripAdvisor shows standard hours and Yelp 'Updated February 2026' label suggests the business is still operating, possibly with seasonal/festival closures
[UNVERIFIED] Whether Ron and Norma Hare are still current operators as of 2026
[UNVERIFIED] Facebook activity recency — page would not return content via WebFetch
Entity confusion risk: two OpenTable listings ('Ciro's Restorante' Italian and 'Ciro's' American) and two Facebook pages exist for what appears to be the same business
